nativeRange.habitat: Grows on rocky subtidal reef substrates, from shallow surge zones down to around 30 m, and is also found free-floating in areas of moderate to high flow.

summary: Dragon's Tongue is a red ornamental macroalgae prized for its deeply lobed, flame-shaped fronds ranging from pink to bright red. Closely related to Dragon's Breath (H. durvillei) and Leafy Ogo (H. maculata), it is generally kept as a display specimen and provides moderate nutrient export.
careNotes:
Dragon's Tongue (Halymenia dilatata) is a red macroalgae prized in the reef aquarium hobby for its deeply lobed, flame-shaped fronds, which range in color from pink to bright red — the origin of its common name. It belongs to the family Halymeniaceae and is closely related to two other ornamental red macroalgae that frequently appear alongside it in the trade: Dragon's Breath (Halymenia durvillei) and Leafy Ogo (Halymenia maculata). All three share a broadly similar appearance, so it is worth noting that some trade sources have applied the Dragon's Tongue name to H. durvillei, which is an Indo-Pacific species; true Dragon's Tongue (H. dilatata) is native to the Gulf of Mexico, Caribbean, and Tropical Atlantic.

In the aquarium, Dragon's Tongue can be kept either free-floating or anchored to live rock. Attached specimens have been reported to reach heights exceeding 45 cm (about 1.5 ft). Like other red macroalgae in this genus, it must not be planted into a sand bed — doing so will cause the base to decay. Instead, secure it to rock using aquarium-safe superglue, zip-ties or fishing line, or by wedging it into a crevice.

Water chemistry should fall within standard reef parameters: alkalinity of 8–12 dKH is recommended, and supplementation with iron, iodine, magnesium, and other trace elements has been reported to support coloration and growth. The species tends to grow at a moderate rate and is generally considered reef-safe, though it is extremely palatable to tangs, rabbitfish, angelfish, and most herbivorous fish, as well as sea urchins; placement away from dedicated herbivores is strongly advised.

Dragon's Tongue is well suited to both display aquariums and refugiums. Its ornamental coloration makes it a standout choice for display use, where it benefits from low to moderate lighting and moderate to high flow. Propagation is straightforward and is accomplished primarily by fragmentation — trimming pieces from an established colony — or by wedging or tying off a section to a new rock.

additional.name: fishy.wiki (Halymenia dilatata)
additional.notes: Cross-checked water parameters. Agreed on SG 1.023–1.025 and kH 8–12; temperature range 20–27°C vs AlgaeBarn 25.6–28.3°C — averaged to 22–27°C. Disagreed on lighting (medium-high vs low) and growth rate (fast vs slow) — medium used as consensus middle value for both.

additional.name: WoRMS (World Register of Marine Species)
additional.notes: Confirmed family Halymeniaceae, order Halymeniales, class Florideophyceae. Synonym Sebdenia dilatata (Zanardini) De Toni, 1900 confirmed unaccepted.

additional.name: AlgaeBarn blog — Red Macroalgae Refugium Guide
additional.notes: Corroborated low-to-moderate lighting, slow growth rate, ornamental and refugium uses. Size (>1.5 ft attached) consistent with main product page.

additional.name: Reeflings — Halymenia floresii care guide (sibling genus)
additional.notes: Used as sibling-species reference for temperature cross-check only (H. floresii: 22–26°C). pH 8.1–8.4 and SG 1.023–1.025 consistent with primary sources.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
